title: Google's Pixel 11 launch event is set for August 12, with possible price increases
gist: Google set a Made By Google event for August 12 to launch the Pixel 11 line, with leaks pointing to across-the-board price increases of roughly $100 driven by an AI-infrastructure-related component shortage, plus a shift to 256GB base storage and a possible move from Qualcomm to Google Tensor silicon in the Pixel Watch.

insights.claim: Rumored Pixel 11 price increases are attributed to an AI-driven component shortage, with numerous phone brands announcing price hikes in 2026 as the AI infrastructure buildout tightens component supply.
insights.speaker: Ryan Whitwam
insights.so_what: AI datacenter demand is spilling into consumer-hardware bills of materials, a margin and pricing headwind across the smartphone sector to watch industry-wide.
insights.quote: Some new rumors, however, suggest the updated phones will come with inflated price tags courtesy of the AI-driven component shortage.
insights.locator: Google has announced its next Made By Google event

insights.claim: The author frames 2026 as materially different from 2025, when the AI infrastructure hype train was only beginning to show impact at the Pixel 10 launch, making a Pixel price bump credible.
insights.speaker: Ryan Whitwam
insights.so_what: Signals that component-cost pressure from AI is intensifying year-over-year, not a one-off, supporting a multi-quarter consumer-electronics inflation thesis.
insights.quote: We were only beginning to see the full impact of the AI infrastructure hype train in 2025 when Google released the Pixel 10 series.
insights.locator: The Pixel 10 series included three flat phones

insights.claim: European leaks put the Pixel 11 at 999 euros and Pixel 11 Pro at 1,199 euros, matching last year's 256GB upgrade prices, but with the cheaper base storage option eliminated the change amounts to a straight price increase.
insights.speaker: Ryan Whitwam
insights.so_what: Removing the low-storage SKU is a stealth price hike mechanism—raising ASPs without headline sticker shock.
insights.locator: The leaks also include alleged pricing

insights.claim: If Google's pricing conversion holds, the Pixel 11 starts at $899 in the US ($100 more than last year), the Pixel 11 Pro at $1,099, the Pixel 11 Pro XL at $1,299, and the Pixel 11 Pro Fold at $1,899.
insights.speaker: Ryan Whitwam
insights.so_what: A ~$100 uplift across the range lifts Google hardware ASPs but risks demand elasticity at flagship price points.
insights.locator: If Google's pricing conversion strategy holds

insights.claim: Google is reportedly dropping the 128GB base storage tier and moving both smaller Pixel phones to 256GB base, citing that people don't upgrade as often, making 128GB inadequate for a modern flagship.
insights.speaker: Ryan Whitwam
insights.so_what: Longer replacement cycles are pushing OEMs to raise baseline specs, increasing component content per unit and reinforcing higher ASPs.
insights.locator: Recent reports claim that Google is finally doing away

insights.claim: The top Pixel 11 Pro Fold could reach $1,899 yet still price below Samsung's flagship foldables, per the author's estimate.
insights.speaker: Ryan Whitwam
insights.so_what: Google positioning foldables as a relative-value alternative to Samsung is a competitive-pricing lever in the premium foldable segment.

insights.claim: The Pixel Watch 5 may move from a Qualcomm Snapdragon chip to a Google-designed Tensor chip, extending Google's vertical silicon integration from phones to wearables.
insights.speaker: Ryan Whitwam
insights.so_what: A Tensor wearable transition would reduce Qualcomm content in Google's lineup; watch battery/power tradeoffs as an execution risk given the rough Tensor phone transition.
insights.quote: The new wearable may move to a Google-designed Tensor chip instead of Qualcomm Snapdragon, and that transition for Google's phones was a bit rough.
insights.locator: Google will likely also reveal the Pixel Watch 5

insights.claim: The most notable Pixel 11 design change is expected to be rumored 'Pixel Glow' rear-facing notification and status lights rather than any major form-factor overhaul.
insights.speaker: Ryan Whitwam
insights.so_what: Incremental hardware differentiation suggests Google is competing on software/AI and pricing rather than industrial design this cycle.
insights.locator: The sleek lines of the phone
